searchDocs.title: What's in a Summary?​
searchDocs.content: A typical AI bill summary includes:  Total amount and breakdownNumber of days delivered/visitedNotable patterns (e.g. "3 days were skipped mid-month")Comparison to previous months (if available)Any anomalies or observations

searchDocs.title: Generating a Summary​
searchDocs.content: Go to BillsClick on a bill to open itClick Generate AI SummaryWait 3–5 seconds for the summary to appear    The summary appears as a card inside the bill, written in plain English. It covers the total charged, notable patterns (e.g. missed days), and a comparison to the previous month where data is available.

searchDocs.title: Example Summary​
searchDocs.content: Morning Milk — January 2026 Your milk was delivered on 27 out of 31 days in January, totalling ₹1,512. The 4 missed days were on 2nd, 14th, 15th, and 28th January. This is slightly lower than your December delivery count of 29 days. Total charge: ₹56/day × 27 days = ₹1,512.

searchDocs.title: Regenerating a Summary​
searchDocs.content: AI summaries are stored with the bill. To refresh:  Open the billClick Regenerate SummaryA new summary is generated using the latest tracking data

searchDocs.title: Cost​
searchDocs.content: Each summary uses a small number of AI tokens (~500–1000 tokens depending on bill complexity). You can monitor your AI usage in Analytics → AI Usage.  tip Generate summaries for your top 3 most expensive services each month. It takes less than a minute and gives you a clear picture of where your money is going.

searchDocs.title: Opening the Agent​
searchDocs.content: Click the indigo chat bubble (🟣) in the bottom-right corner of any page to open the Agent panel.

searchDocs.title: Chatting with the Agent​
searchDocs.content: Type your question or command in the chat input. The Agent understands natural language and responds instantly.

searchDocs.title: Answer Questions​
searchDocs.content: "What services do I have?""How much is my total bill this month?""Is my internet marked active for February?"

searchDocs.title: Take Actions (with Confirmation)​
searchDocs.content: The Agent can perform write actions on your account — generating bills, creating or editing services, marking deliveries, and more. Every action shows a confirmation card before anything is saved.  See Agent Actions for the full list with examples and tips:  Generate BillCreate ServiceEdit ServiceToggle Service Active/InactiveMark Bill as PaidUpdate Calendar Day

searchDocs.title: Conversation History​
searchDocs.content: Click the history icon (🕐) in the Agent panel to see past conversations. Each conversation is automatically titled based on its content.

searchDocs.title: Agent Thinking Mode​
searchDocs.content: When you ask the Agent a complex question (e.g. "Which service cost me the most across all of last year?"), it may enter Thinking Mode. You'll see a reasoning indicator while the Agent works through the problem. This produces more accurate, context-aware answers but takes a few extra seconds.

searchDocs.title: Agent Analytics​
searchDocs.content: Each Agent response has a small 📊 Analytics icon. Click it to see token usage and estimated cost for that specific message — useful if you're monitoring your AI provider spend.

searchDocs.title: How It Differs from Ask AI​
searchDocs.content: Feature	Ask AI	AgentChat interface	Full page	Floating popup History	Full panel	Recent sessions Actions	No	Yes, with confirmation Best for	Deep analysis	Quick tasks while browsing  note The Agent is powered by the same AI provider you've configured. If AI is not set up, the Agent will prompt you to configure it.

searchDocs.title: Spending Analytics​
searchDocs.content: The Analytics page shows your household spending broken down by service and month. You can use this to:  See total spend across all services for any given monthCompare month-over-month spending trendsIdentify your most expensive services at a glanceSpot months where spending was unusually high or low

searchDocs.title: Accessing Analytics​
searchDocs.content: Click Analytics in the sidebarThe default view shows the current month with a chart of service-wise spendUse the month/year selector to browse historical data

searchDocs.title: Charts Available​
searchDocs.content: Chart	What It ShowsMonthly Total	Total spend across all services per month By Service	Breakdown of costs per individual service By Type	Grouped spend by service type (Home Delivery, Utility, etc.) Trend Line	6-month rolling average of your household spend

searchDocs.title: AI Usage Analytics​
searchDocs.content: The AI Usage tab within Analytics tracks your AI API consumption across all AI-powered features in YesBill.

searchDocs.title: What's Tracked​
searchDocs.content: Total tokens used — Combined prompt + completion tokensEstimated cost — Based on your AI provider's pricingUsage by feature — Ask AI chat, Agent, Bill SummariesUsage by model — Breakdown per AI model usedDaily/Monthly trends — See when you use AI the most

searchDocs.title: How to Monitor Costs​
searchDocs.content: Go to Analytics → AI Usage tabReview the total tokens and estimated cost for the current monthUse the model breakdown to see if a cheaper model can handle your queries  tip If AI costs are higher than expected, switch to a smaller model (e.g. GPT-4o Mini or Gemini 2.0 Flash) for routine Ask AI queries, and save the larger models for complex bill analysis.  note Cost estimates are approximate and based on public pricing for each AI provider. Actual charges may vary. Always check your AI provider's billing dashboard for exact costs.

searchDocs.title: Generate Bill​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to generate a monthly bill for one or more of your services.  Example prompts:  "Generate my February bill""Create a bill for milk and tiffin for January 2026""Generate bill for all services last month and email it to me"  What the Agent collects before generating:  Which services to include (by name or "all")Which month (YYYY-MM)Whether to send the bill to your email  Confirmation card shows:  Estimated total (calculated from your calendar records, no AI needed for the estimate)Month and included services  After confirming:  A full AI-powered bill is generated with insights and recommendationsA notification is sent: "Bill generated"If you requested email, the bill is sent to your account email (respects your notification preferences)  note The estimated total on the confirmation card is a quick estimate. The final bill total uses AI analysis of your delivery records and may differ slightly.

searchDocs.title: Create Service​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to add a new service to your account without going to the Services page.  Example prompts:  "Add a new milk service, ₹30 per day, home delivery""Create a Netflix subscription, ₹649 per month""Add an electricity service, utility type"  Required fields the Agent will ask for:  Field	DescriptionName	Service name (e.g., "Milk", "Tiffin", "Netflix") Price	Amount per billing cycle Delivery type	home_delivery, subscription, utility, payment, or visit_based  Optional fields (Agent infers sensible defaults):  Frequency type (daily/weekly/monthly)Schedule (morning/afternoon/evening)IconNotesBilling dayStart / end date  Confirmation card shows: All fields in a mini table — Name, Price, Type, Schedule, and any extras.  After confirming:  Service is created as an active consumer-role serviceA notification is sent: "New Service Added"Service immediately appears in your Services page  info Provider-role service creation (for service providers managing multiple customers) is a planned feature — see the Roadmap.

searchDocs.title: Edit Service​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to update one or more fields of an existing service.  Example prompts:  "Change milk price to ₹35""Update my tiffin schedule to morning and change the notes to 'lunch only on weekdays'""Rename Netflix to Netflix Premium and set price to ₹799"  How the Agent finds your service:Just say the service name — the Agent matches it directly. No need to look up IDs.  Editable fields:  Field	Descriptionname	Rename the service price	New price notes	Update description/notes billing_day	Day of month for billing schedule	morning / afternoon / evening / anytime icon	Icon identifier type	daily / weekly / monthly / custom delivery_type	Change delivery/billing model start_date	Start date (YYYY-MM-DD) end_date	End date (YYYY-MM-DD)  Confirmation card shows: Only the fields being changed, with old value → new value for each.  After confirming:  All specified fields are updated atomicallyA notification is sent: "Service Updated: [Service Name]"  tip You can change multiple fields at once in a single message. The Agent collects everything before showing the confirmation card.

searchDocs.title: Toggle Service Active / Inactive​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to activate or deactivate a service.  Example prompts:  "Deactivate my milk service — I'm going on vacation""Reactivate tiffin service""Turn off Netflix for now"  How it works:  The Agent asks (or infers from context) which service to toggleInactive services are hidden from the daily calendar and won't be included in auto-generated billsYou can reactivate at any time  After confirming:  Service active state is updatedA notification is sent: "Service Activated" or "Service Deactivated"

searchDocs.title: Mark Bill as Paid / Unpaid​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to record a payment for a generated bill.  Example prompts:  "Mark my January bill as paid""I paid the February bill via UPI""Mark the milk bill unpaid — payment bounced"  What the Agent collects:  Which bill (by month or bill title)Paid or unpaidPayment method (optional — e.g., "UPI", "cash", "bank transfer")  After confirming:  Bill payment status is updatedIf marked paid, a notification is sent: "Payment Recorded"

searchDocs.title: Update Calendar Day​
searchDocs.content: Ask the Agent to mark a service delivery for a specific past day.  Example prompts:  "Mark today's milk as delivered""Yesterday's tiffin was skipped""Mark 2026-03-05 electricity as delivered""Today's newspaper — mark as not delivered"  Rules:  Only dates within the last 30 days (including today) can be updatedFuture dates are not allowedAccepted date formats: today, yesterday, YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYYFor multiple dates, mention them all — the Agent handles each one  Status values:  Status	Meaningdelivered	Service was received/used skipped	Service was skipped/not needed not_delivered	Service was not delivered  After confirming:  Calendar record is updatedAffects your monthly bill calculation for quantity-based services

searchDocs.title: Confirmation Card​
searchDocs.content: Every action above shows a confirmation card in the chat before applying changes:  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ⚠️ Action Required │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────│ │ Edit 'Milk' — update: Price, Schedule │ │ │ │ PRICE ₹30.00 → ₹35.00 │ │ SCHEDULE Morning → Evening │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────│ │ [ Cancel ] [ Confirm Changes ] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   Cancel — discards the action, nothing is changedConfirm Changes — applies the action immediatelyIf anything goes wrong, an error message appears inline with a chance to retry

searchDocs.title: Tips & Best Practices​
searchDocs.content: Be specific with service names — say "milk" not "my morning delivery" to avoid ambiguityBatch calendar updates — "mark Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday milk as delivered" works in one messageCheck the diff before confirming — the confirmation card shows exactly what will changeAgent remembers context — within the same conversation you don't need to repeat service names

searchDocs.title: 🗣️ Ask AI​
searchDocs.content: A chat interface where you can ask questions about your bills and services in plain English (or Hindi!).  Examples:  "How much did I spend on household services in January?""Did the maid come every day last week?""Which service costs the most each month?"  → Learn more about Ask AI

searchDocs.title: 🤖 AI Agent (Floating Assistant)​
searchDocs.content: An Intercom-style floating chat button that lets you manage services with natural language commands.  Examples:  "Mark today's milk as delivered""Mark yesterday's tiffin as skipped""What services do I have?"  The Agent performs actions after your confirmation — no accidental changes.  → Learn more about the Agent

searchDocs.title: 📝 AI Bill Summaries​
searchDocs.content: After bills are generated, AI can write a plain-English summary of each bill explaining the charges.  → Learn more about AI Bill Generation

searchDocs.title: Requirements​
searchDocs.content: All AI features require an API key from at least one provider:  Provider	Where to Get KeyOpenAI	platform.openai.com Anthropic	console.anthropic.com Google Gemini	aistudio.google.com  → Set up your AI provider  note You pay your AI provider directly based on usage. YesBill does not charge extra for AI features.

searchDocs.title: Opening Ask AI​
searchDocs.content: Click Ask AI in the sidebarType your question in the chat inputPress Enter or click Send

searchDocs.title: Asking Questions​
searchDocs.content: Ask AI understands natural language. Simply type as you would to a person.

searchDocs.title: Bill Queries​
searchDocs.content: "How much did I spend on all services in February?""What's my total milk bill for this year?""Did I pay the internet bill in December?"

searchDocs.title: Service Tracking​
searchDocs.content: "How many days was the tiffin delivered in January?""Did the maid miss any days last week?""Which services are currently active?"

searchDocs.title: Spending Analysis​
searchDocs.content: "What's my most expensive service?""How has my monthly spend changed over the last 6 months?""Break down my household expenses by category"

searchDocs.title: General Help​
searchDocs.content: "How do I add a new service?""What's the difference between Visit-based and Home Delivery?"

searchDocs.title: Chat Options & Conversations​
searchDocs.content: Each conversation is saved automatically. The left panel shows your conversation history — click any past conversation to resume it.    Right-click or hover over a conversation in the history list to access options: Rename or Delete a conversation.

searchDocs.title: Per-Message Features​
searchDocs.content: Each AI response has a set of action buttons below it:  Button	What It Does📊 Analytics	Shows token usage and cost for that specific message 👍 / 👎 Feedback	Rate the response quality (helps improve the AI) 📋 Copy	Copy the full response text to your clipboard

searchDocs.title: Model Selection​
searchDocs.content: Use the model selector above the chat input to choose which AI model handles your query. Available models are based on which API keys you've configured:  OpenAI — GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, GPT-4.1Anthropic — Claude 3.7 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 HaikuGoogle — Gemini 2.0 Flash, Gemini 1.5 Pro  Switch models freely between messages — each conversation remembers which model was used for each response.

searchDocs.title: Reasoning Effort​
searchDocs.content: For models that support extended thinking (Claude 3.7 Sonnet, o3-mini), a Reasoning selector appears next to the model picker:  Level	Speed	Depth	Use WhenLow	Fastest	Surface	Quick factual lookups Medium	Balanced	Standard	Most everyday questions High	Slowest	Deep	Complex multi-month analysis

searchDocs.title: Token Usage & Thinking Analytics​
searchDocs.content: Click the 📊 icon on any message to open the Analytics panel showing:  Model used and providerPrompt tokens and completion tokensEstimated cost for that query    When using a reasoning model (High effort), the analytics panel also shows the thinking token count — the internal reasoning steps the model performed before responding. Thinking tokens consume more budget but produce more accurate, nuanced answers.  tip Ask AI has access to all your YesBill data — services, tracking history, bills, and payments. The more data you've entered, the better the answers.

searchDocs.title: Supported Providers​
searchDocs.content: Provider	Models Available	Best ForOpenAI	GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, GPT-4.1	Speed and reliability Anthropic	Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Haiku	Detailed analysis Google	Gemini 2.0 Flash, Gemini 1.5 Pro	Budget-friendly

searchDocs.title: OpenAI​
searchDocs.content: Visit platform.openai.comSign up or log inGo to API Keys → Create new secret keyCopy the key (starts with sk-...)

searchDocs.title: Anthropic​
searchDocs.content: Visit console.anthropic.comSign up or log inGo to API Keys → Create KeyCopy the key

searchDocs.title: Google Gemini​
searchDocs.content: Visit aistudio.google.comClick Get API KeyCopy the key

searchDocs.title: Adding Your Key to YesBill​
searchDocs.content: Go to Settings → AI Configuration to see the full configuration panel. From here you can manage providers, models, reasoning settings, and AI insights.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Choose a Provider​
searchDocs.content: Select your preferred AI provider by clicking its tab (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google). Then paste your API key into the field provided and click Validate to verify it works.  Go to Settings → AI ConfigurationSelect your provider tabPaste your API keyClick Validate to test the keyClick Save

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — Select a Default Model​
searchDocs.content: After adding your API key, choose the default model you want YesBill to use for all AI features. The available models update automatically based on your validated provider.  tip Start with OpenAI GPT-4o Mini or Google Gemini 2.0 Flash for low-cost usage while you're getting started.

searchDocs.title: Step 3 — Set Default Reasoning Effort​
searchDocs.content: For models that support extended thinking (like Claude 3.7 Sonnet or o3-mini), you can set the default reasoning effort:  Level	Behaviour	Best ForLow	Fast, minimal reasoning	Quick bill lookups Medium	Balanced speed and depth	General Ask AI use High	Deep reasoning, slower	Complex spending analysis  Choose Medium as a sensible default for most households.

searchDocs.title: Step 4 — Enable or Disable AI Insights​
searchDocs.content: The AI Insights toggle controls whether YesBill automatically surfaces AI-generated insights on your Dashboard and Bills pages (e.g. "Your milk spend increased 12% this month"). Toggle it off if you prefer a cleaner interface or want to reduce AI API usage.

searchDocs.title: Multiple Providers​
searchDocs.content: You can add keys for all three providers and switch between them in Ask AI using the model selector. Each provider's key is stored separately and securely.

searchDocs.title: Security​
searchDocs.content: Your API keys are stored only in your browser session and sent directly to the AI provider. YesBill never stores, logs, or shares your API keys on any server or database. Your keys remain private and under your control at all times.

searchDocs.title: Removing a Key​
searchDocs.content: Go to Settings → AI ConfigurationClick Remove next to the providerConfirm the removal  After removing all keys, AI features will be disabled until you add a new key.

searchDocs.title: When Are Bills Generated?​
searchDocs.content: Bills are generated automatically:  On the 1st of each month — for all services from the previous monthVia the backend cron job — runs daily at midnight and checks for any missing bills  You can also manually trigger generation from the Bills page.

searchDocs.title: Bill Generation Email​
searchDocs.content: When YesBill generates a new bill, it sends you an email notification summarising the bill amount, the service it applies to, and the billing period. Click the link in the email to open the full bill directly. Make sure Bill Generated notifications are enabled in Settings → Notifications.

searchDocs.title: How Generation Works​
searchDocs.content: YesBill looks at your tracking data for the monthFor day-tracked services: counts Delivered/Visited daysFor utility services: checks if Active or InactiveFor fixed services: uses the configured monthly priceCreates a bill record with the calculated amountIf AI is configured, the AI generates a summary (optional)

searchDocs.title: Manual Generation​
searchDocs.content: To generate a bill immediately:  Go to BillsSelect the month from the month picker at the topChoose one or more services from the service selectorOptionally add Bill Notes (e.g. "Vacation week excluded")Click Generate Bill — the AI creates an itemised bill with an optional summary

searchDocs.title: What If Data Is Missing?​
searchDocs.content: If you forgot to mark some days:  Those days remain Pending and are excluded from the billYou can still go back and mark them — then regenerate the billUse Ask AI to ask "Did I miss any days in January?" to find gaps

searchDocs.title: Regenerating Bills​
searchDocs.content: If you mark more days after a bill is generated, you can regenerate:  Open the billClick RegenerateThe bill total is recalculated with updated data  warning Regenerating a bill will overwrite the existing total. If you've already marked the bill as Paid, be careful before regenerating.

searchDocs.title: Browsing History​
searchDocs.content: Go to Bills in the sidebarUse the month/year selector at the top to navigate to past monthsAll bills for that month are listed below  Each past bill card shows the service name, billing period, total amount, and paid/unpaid status.

searchDocs.title: Filtering Bills​
searchDocs.content: Use the filters to narrow down:  By status: Paid / Unpaid / AllBy service: Show bills for a specific service onlyBy type: Filter by service type (Home Delivery, Subscription, etc.)

searchDocs.title: Bill Detail View​
searchDocs.content: Click any bill to see the full breakdown:  Line items for each day (for tracked services)AI-generated summary (if available)Payment details (method, note, date paid)Bill generation timestamp

searchDocs.title: Export & Download​
searchDocs.content: You can download any bill as a PDF or CSV file directly from the bill detail view.

searchDocs.title: How to Export​
searchDocs.content: Open any bill from the Bills pageClick the Download or Export button (top right of the bill)Choose your format: PDF — formatted bill document, ready to share with your vendor or accountantCSV — raw data export, useful for importing into spreadsheets The file downloads to your device automatically  tip Use PDF exports to settle disputes with vendors — it includes a timestamped breakdown of every tracked day.

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: Use Ask AI to query history in plain English: "How much did I spend on tiffin in November?""Which months did I pay my internet bill late?""Total household spend in Q1 2026?" The Analytics dashboard shows monthly spend trends as charts

searchDocs.title: Data Retention​
searchDocs.content: All bill data is stored indefinitely in your Supabase account. Your history is available as long as your account exists.

searchDocs.title: How to Mark a Bill as Paid​
From the Bills Page​
searchDocs.content: Go to Bills in the sidebarFind the bill you've paidClick Mark as PaidA modal opens:    Select your payment methodOptionally add a payment note (e.g. UPI transaction ID)  Click Confirm

searchDocs.title: Paid Bill Status​
searchDocs.content: Once confirmed, the bill card updates to show a green Paid badge along with the payment method and the date you recorded the payment.

searchDocs.title: From the Calendar​
searchDocs.content: Open the Service Calendar for a subscription/payment serviceClick the billing day cellClick Mark Paid in the popup

searchDocs.title: Payment Methods​
searchDocs.content: You can record how you paid:  Method	ExamplesCash	Hand-to-hand cash payment UPI	GPay, PhonePe, Paytm, BHIM Bank Transfer	NEFT, RTGS, IMPS Cheque	Cheque payment Card	Debit/credit card Auto-debit	Bank auto-debit or SI

searchDocs.title: Undoing a Payment​
searchDocs.content: To unmark a bill as paid:  Open the billClick Undo Payment (shown when bill is marked Paid)Confirm the action

searchDocs.title: Payment History​
searchDocs.content: All payment records are stored permanently. You can:  Filter bills by "Paid" or "Unpaid" statusSee payment method and notes on each billUse Ask AI to query payment history ("Did I pay the internet bill in December?")

searchDocs.title: What's in a Bill?​
searchDocs.content: Each bill contains:  Service name and typeBilling period (e.g. January 2026)Line items — each tracked day or chargeTotal amount in ₹Payment status — Paid or UnpaidAI Summary (if AI is configured) — a plain-English description of the charges

searchDocs.title: Bill Structure by Service Type​
searchDocs.content: Service Type	Bill Items	Total CalculationHome Delivery	Days marked Delivered	Count × Daily Rate Visit-based	Days marked Visited	Count × Daily Rate Utility	Active/Inactive for month	Monthly Rate or ₹0 Subscription	Fixed monthly charge	Monthly Rate Payment	Fixed EMI/due amount	Monthly Rate

searchDocs.title: Where to Find Bills​
searchDocs.content: Go to Bills in the sidebarUse the month/year selector to browse bills by periodClick any bill to see the full breakdown

searchDocs.title: Generated Bill Details​
searchDocs.content: Clicking into an individual bill shows the full breakdown: each line item, the daily rate or fixed charge, and the total amount. If AI is configured, you can generate a plain-English summary directly from this screen.

searchDocs.title: Bill with AI Summary​
searchDocs.content: When an AI summary has been generated for a bill, it appears as a highlighted card at the top of the bill detail view — giving you an at-a-glance plain-English explanation of all charges before diving into the line items.

searchDocs.title: Bill Status​
searchDocs.content: Unpaid 🔴 — Bill has been generated but not yet paidPaid 🟢 — You've recorded payment for this bill

searchDocs.title: AI Bill Summary​
searchDocs.content: If you have AI configured, each bill can have an AI-generated summary that explains:  What was charged and whyUnusual months (e.g. "Only 18 days delivered — 3 days missed")Total spend in plain language  Click Generate Summary on any bill to create one.

searchDocs.title: Accessing the Yearly View​
searchDocs.content: Click any service name in the sidebar or Calendar viewOn the Service Calendar page, click the Yearly tab at the top

searchDocs.title: What You See​
searchDocs.content: A grid of all 12 months with colour-coded payment status:  Colour	Status🟢 Green	Bill paid 🔴 Red	Bill unpaid or overdue ⬜ Grey	No bill generated yet

searchDocs.title: Use Cases​
searchDocs.content: EMI tracking: Verify you haven't missed a single loan paymentSubscription review: See which months a streaming service was paidAnnual audit: Quickly spot any unpaid bills from the yearExpense history: Get a bird's eye view of the year's spending

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: Click any month cell to jump to that month's bill detailThe yearly view is especially useful for Payment and Subscription types where you want to verify payment continuity

searchDocs.title: Monthly Calendar View​
searchDocs.content: The default view shows all services in a grid of days. Each service has a row, and each day has a status indicator:  🟢 Delivered / Visited — Service was received/provided🔴 Skipped / Missed — Service did not happen⬜ Pending — Not yet marked (default)🔵 Paid — Bill is paid (for subscription/payment types)

searchDocs.title: Weekly Calendar View​
searchDocs.content: Switch to the Weekly tab to see a condensed 7-day view. This is useful for a quick check of the current week without the visual noise of the full month.

searchDocs.title: Service Calendar (Per-Service View)​
searchDocs.content: Click on any service name to open its dedicated calendar page with:  Day-by-day attendance grid (for delivery/visit types)Monthly active toggle (for utility types)Billing day card with payment status (for subscription/payment types)Yearly view — 12-month paid grid overview

searchDocs.title: Service Details Panel​
searchDocs.content: Within the service calendar, clicking a specific day or the service details section opens a Service Details panel that shows:  Service name, type, and daily/monthly rateCurrent month's tracking summary (days delivered, days skipped, pending days)Total bill amount accrued so far this monthA direct link to the full bill for this service

searchDocs.title: Navigation​
searchDocs.content: Use the ← → arrows to move between monthsClick Today to jump to the current monthUse the service filter to show/hide specific services

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: Mark yesterday's status first thing in the morning — it takes 5 secondsUse the keyboard shortcut D for Delivered and S for Skipped (when a day is focused)The calendar remembers your scroll position when you navigate between months

searchDocs.title: From the Calendar View​
searchDocs.content: Navigate to the current month using the Calendar in the sidebarFind the row for your service (e.g. "Morning Milk")Click the day cell — a date modal opensSelect Delivered or Skipped (for home delivery) / Visited or Missed (for visit-based)Click Save to confirm the status

searchDocs.title: From the Service Calendar Page​
searchDocs.content: Click a service name to open its individual calendarClick any day in the attendance gridThe day toggles between states with each click

searchDocs.title: Tracking History​
searchDocs.content: As you mark days throughout the month, the calendar fills in with colour-coded status indicators. Green cells are delivered/visited days, red cells are skipped/missed days, and grey cells are still pending. This filled-in view makes it easy to spot any gaps before the month ends.

searchDocs.title: Status States​
searchDocs.content: Status	Colour	MeaningPending	Grey	Not yet marked Delivered / Visited	Green	Service happened Skipped / Missed	Red	Service didn't happen  note Pending days are excluded from the bill calculation — only Delivered/Visited days count towards your bill.

searchDocs.title: Editing Past Days​
searchDocs.content: You can mark or edit any day in the current and previous months. To go back further, navigate to the previous month using the arrow buttons.

searchDocs.title: Bulk Marking​
searchDocs.content: Coming soon: ability to mark multiple days at once (e.g. mark all Sundays as Skipped).

searchDocs.title: Best Practices​
searchDocs.content: Daily habit: Mark yesterday's status when you sit down in the morningMonthly habit: Before month end, check for any Pending days and mark themDon't worry about errors: You can always click a marked day to change its status

searchDocs.title: The Login Screen​
searchDocs.content: When you first visit the YesBill app, you'll land on the login screen. From here you can sign in with an existing account or navigate to sign up.

searchDocs.title: Sign Up with Email​
searchDocs.content: Visit the YesBill app at ishan96dev.github.io/YesBillClick Sign Up on the login pageEnter your email address and choose a strong password Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, lowercase letter, number, and symbol Click Create AccountCheck your inbox for a confirmation email from YesBillClick the confirmation link to verify your email address

searchDocs.title: Confirmation Email​
searchDocs.content: After registering, you'll receive an email like the one above. Click the Confirm your email button to activate your account. Check your spam folder if it doesn't arrive within a few minutes.

searchDocs.title: Sign In with Google (SSO)​
searchDocs.content: YesBill supports Single Sign-On (SSO) via Google. No password is needed — Google authenticates you directly.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Click Continue with Google​
searchDocs.content: On the login page, click Continue with Google. A Google account picker opens. Select the Google account you want to use.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — Authorise YesBill​
searchDocs.content: You may see a permissions screen asking you to authorise YesBill to access your email address. Click Allow. You'll be redirected back to YesBill and logged in automatically.  tip SSO accounts don't have a YesBill password. To change your login password, do it from your Google Account settings.

searchDocs.title: First Login​
searchDocs.content: After signing in for the first time, you'll be taken through the Onboarding flow to set up your profile and optionally configure an AI provider.  tip Use a strong, unique password for your YesBill account. You can change it later from Settings → Security.

searchDocs.title: Forgot Your Password?​
searchDocs.content: If you can't remember your login password, YesBill provides a secure self-service reset flow.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Click "Forgot Password"​
searchDocs.content: On the login screen, click the Forgot password? link. You'll be taken to the password reset page where you enter your registered email address.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — Submit Your Email​
searchDocs.content: Enter your email and click Send Reset Link. You'll see a confirmation message that a reset email has been sent.

searchDocs.title: Step 3 — Check Your Inbox​
searchDocs.content: Open the email and click the Reset Password button. The link is valid for 24 hours — check your spam folder if it doesn't arrive within a few minutes.

searchDocs.title: Step 4 — Set a New Password​
searchDocs.content: Enter your new password (and confirm it), then click Update Password. You'll be redirected to the login page to sign in with your new credentials.

searchDocs.title: Other Login Issues​
searchDocs.content: Email not verified? Check your spam folder for the original confirmation emailAccount locked? Contact support at support@yesbill.com

searchDocs.title: Dashboard Overview​
searchDocs.content: The Dashboard is your command centre for everything in YesBill. It surfaces the most important information at a glance so you always know where things stand.

searchDocs.title: Updated Dashboard​
searchDocs.content: As you add more services and track more months, the Dashboard becomes richer — showing trends, recent activity across multiple services, and smarter AI-powered insights.

searchDocs.title: Key Metrics (KPI Cards)​
searchDocs.content: The top row of cards shows your most important numbers for the current month:  Total This Month — Sum of all generated bills for the current monthUnpaid Bills — Number of bills that haven't been marked as paidServices Active — Count of live services being trackedDays Tracked — Total number of days marked across all services this month

searchDocs.title: Recent Bills​
searchDocs.content: A list of the most recently generated bills. Click any bill to open its full detail view.

searchDocs.title: Services Summary​
searchDocs.content: A quick summary of all your active services with their current month status — useful for a quick sanity check without navigating to the full Services or Calendar page.

searchDocs.title: Quick Actions​
searchDocs.content: Common shortcuts available directly from the Dashboard:  Mark Today's Services — Jump to the Calendar for today's dateGenerate Bills — Trigger bill generation for the current monthAsk AI — Open the Ask AI chat from the Dashboard

searchDocs.title: Navigation​
searchDocs.content: Use the sidebar on the left to navigate to all sections of the app:  Section	What It DoesDashboard	This overview screen Calendar	Day-by-day service tracking Services	Manage your service list Bills	View and pay your bills Analytics	Charts and spending trends Ask AI	Natural language queries Settings	Profile, notifications, security  tip Bookmark the YesBill app URL so you can open it quickly each morning and mark yesterday's services in under 30 seconds.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Open the Add Service Form​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services in the sidebar and click + Add Service (top right) to open the service creation form.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — Enter Service Details​
searchDocs.content: Fill in the service details:  Service Name — e.g. "Morning Milk", "Tiffin Service", "Maid"Service Type — Choose the billing model that matches your service (see the table below)Daily Rate / Monthly Price — The cost per day or per monthBilling Day — Day of the month bills are due (for subscription/payment types)

searchDocs.title: Step 3 — Configure Billing Options​
searchDocs.content: Depending on the service type, additional fields may appear such as start date, billing cycle day, or tracking method. Fill these in as appropriate.

searchDocs.title: Step 4 — Save and Confirm​
searchDocs.content: Click Save. Your service is now active and will appear on the Calendar immediately.

searchDocs.title: Choosing the Right Service Type​
searchDocs.content: You're Tracking	Use This TypeDaily milk, newspaper, tiffin	Home Delivery Maid, cook, gardener visits	Visit-based Internet, DTH, electricity	Utility Netflix, Spotify, gym	Subscription Loan EMI, credit card	Payment

searchDocs.title: Example: Tiffin Service​
searchDocs.content: Name: Morning TiffinType: Home DeliveryDaily Rate: ₹80/dayTracking: Mark each day as Delivered or Skipped  The calendar will show this service daily. At month end, your bill will be calculated as ₹80 × days delivered.

searchDocs.title: What Happens Next?​
searchDocs.content: Once a service is added:  It appears on your Calendar for the current monthYou can start marking days (delivered/visited/active)At month end, bills are auto-generated based on your tracking  tip Add all your regular household services at once — the more you track, the more accurate your monthly bill summary will be.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Profile Setup​
searchDocs.content: Fill in your basic profile information:  Full Name — Your name as it appears in the appDisplay Name — Short name used in greetings (optional)Phone Number — For WhatsApp bill notifications (optional)Country & Timezone — Used to display dates correctlyProfile Photo — Upload an avatar image (optional)  Click Save & Continue when done.  info You can update all of this later from Settings → Profile.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — AI Provider Setup​
searchDocs.content: YesBill's AI features (bill summaries, Ask AI chat, Agent assistant) require an API key from an AI provider.

searchDocs.title: Supported Providers​
searchDocs.content: Provider	Good ForOpenAI	Best overall, fast responses Anthropic Claude	Best for detailed analysis Google Gemini	Budget-friendly option

searchDocs.title: How to Set Up​
searchDocs.content: Select your preferred provider (e.g. OpenAI)Enter your API key from the provider's developer consoleChoose a default modelClick Validate to verify the key worksClick Save & Go to Dashboard

searchDocs.title: Skip for Now​
searchDocs.content: If you're not ready to set up AI, click Skip for now. A confirmation modal appears reminding you that AI features will be unavailable until you add a key. Click Skip Anyway to go directly to the Dashboard.  You can configure AI any time later from Settings → AI Configuration.  note AI features won't work until you add an API key. Bill tracking, calendar, and manual billing work without AI.

searchDocs.title: What Is YesBill?​
searchDocs.content: Most Indian households use multiple daily services — morning milk delivery, newspaper, tiffin service, maid, internet, DTH, and more. Keeping track of all of these manually in a notebook or spreadsheet is tedious and error-prone.  YesBill solves this by giving you:  A digital record of every delivery, visit, and paymentAutomatic bill generation at the end of each monthA calendar view to see your entire month at a glanceAI chat to ask questions about your bills in plain English

searchDocs.title: Who Is This For?​
searchDocs.content: YesBill is built for:  Families that use daily home delivery services (milk, newspaper, tiffin)Homes with domestic helpers paid on a visit basisAnyone who wants to track monthly subscriptions and utility bills in one place

searchDocs.title: Key Concepts​
searchDocs.content: Term	MeaningService	A recurring household expense (e.g. "Morning Milk", "Tiffin Service") Service Type	The billing model: Home Delivery, Visit-based, Utility, Subscription, or Payment Calendar	Day-by-day view to mark deliveries/visits Bill	Auto-generated monthly summary of what you owe AI Features	Chat assistant and bill analysis powered by OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google

searchDocs.title: Getting Started​
searchDocs.content: Create your accountComplete onboarding — set up your profile and AI providerAdd your first serviceStart tracking — use the calendar to mark days and let YesBill generate your bills    Note: YesBill currently works best on desktop browsers. Mobile support is coming soon.

searchDocs.title: Pin Conversations​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  Allow users to pin important Ask AI conversations to the top of the history list for quick access. Currently, conversations can only be renamed or deleted.

searchDocs.title: Regenerate Response​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  A Regenerate button on each AI response to re-run the same query and get a fresh answer — useful when a response is cut off or you want to try a different phrasing. Currently, you need to re-send the message manually.

searchDocs.title: Semantic Search for AI Documentation​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  Upgrade the current keyword-based documentation search (which already injects relevant YesBill docs into your AI chat) to a fully semantic, vector-based search. This will improve relevance for natural-language questions and multi-topic queries.

searchDocs.title: Agent: Create & Manage Services for Provider Role​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  Extend the AI Agent to support Provider-role service creation and management. Currently the Agent can create consumer-facing services. Provider-side workflows — creating services for multiple customers, setting up billing templates, and managing customer accounts — require a dedicated UI and permission model being built as part of the Service Provider Portal.

searchDocs.title: Bulk Day Marking​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  Mark multiple days at once — for example, mark all Sundays as Skipped for a service that doesn't operate on weekends. Currently each day must be marked individually.

searchDocs.title: Export as PDF / CSV​
searchDocs.content: Status: ✅ Shipped  Download any bill or a month's summary as a PDF or CSV file — available now from the bill detail view. See Bill History for instructions.

searchDocs.title: Mobile App​
searchDocs.content: Status: Under Consideration  A native mobile app (iOS / Android) or a progressive web app (PWA) for easier on-the-go tracking. Currently YesBill works best on desktop browsers.

searchDocs.title: WhatsApp Notifications​
searchDocs.content: Status: Planned  Receive bill generation alerts and daily service reminders directly on WhatsApp. The phone number field in your profile is reserved for this feature.    Last updated: March 2026

searchDocs.title: Landing page & sign-up​
searchDocs.content: The onboarding experience starts at the landing page, which introduces YesBill's key features before directing new users to sign up.    New users create an account with their email and password. A confirmation email is sent immediately to verify the address before the account is activated.

searchDocs.title: Email & password login​
searchDocs.content: Returning users sign in from the login screen with email and password.

searchDocs.title: Google SSO​
searchDocs.content: Sign in with a Google account using OAuth. No password required — your Google identity is linked to your YesBill profile automatically.

searchDocs.title: Forgot password​
searchDocs.content: A guided four-step reset flow: enter your email → receive the reset email → open the link → set a new password.

searchDocs.title: Onboarding​
searchDocs.content: First-time users are walked through a two-step setup wizard before they reach the dashboard. Both steps can be skipped and completed later from Settings.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Profile setup​
searchDocs.content: Set your display name, avatar, cover image, and timezone so your bills and calendar are always in sync with your local time.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — AI configuration​
searchDocs.content: Connect an AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Gemini) to unlock Ask AI, the AI Agent, and AI-powered bill summaries. You can skip this step and configure it later.

searchDocs.title: Skip modal​
searchDocs.content: A confirmation dialog explains what you'll miss if you skip, making it easy to decide whether to set up now or later.

searchDocs.title: Dashboard​
searchDocs.content: The dashboard is your home screen — a quick-glance summary of pending bills, active services, and overall monthly spend.    The updated dashboard layout adds prominent KPI cards for total spend, active services, pending dues, and bills this month, with quick-access navigation to the most important sections.

searchDocs.title: Services​
searchDocs.content: Services are the core of YesBill. Each service represents something you track and pay for every month.

searchDocs.title: Service overview page​
searchDocs.content: All your services are displayed as cards with their name, type, current amount, and quick-access controls.

searchDocs.title: Five service types​
searchDocs.content: Type	How it worksHome Delivery	Tracks daily deliveries — Delivered, Skipped, or Missed — and bills per unit delivered Visit-based	Tracks visits by a person (e.g., maid, driver) — Visited or Skipped Utility	Monthly active/inactive toggle for fixed-cost utilities (electricity, water, gas) Subscription	Fixed monthly charge on a set billing day Payment	Custom recurring payment on a fixed date

searchDocs.title: Adding a new service​
searchDocs.content: A multi-step wizard guides you through creating any service type — name, icon, amount, billing day, and type-specific settings.

searchDocs.title: Editing and managing services​
searchDocs.content: Edit any service's details, or use the manage panel to reorder, pause, or delete services without affecting historical bill data.

searchDocs.title: Calendar​
searchDocs.content: The calendar is where you log daily activity for your services. It provides monthly, weekly, yearly, and per-service views.

searchDocs.title: Main calendar (monthly view)​
searchDocs.content: The default calendar shows all services across the current month in a compact grid. Each day displays the tracking status for every active service.

searchDocs.title: Per-service calendar​
searchDocs.content: Each service has its own dedicated calendar page. Switch between month, week, and year views, and log status directly from any day cell.

searchDocs.title: Weekly view​
searchDocs.content: The weekly view zooms into a 7-day window for fine-grained day-by-day tracking.

searchDocs.title: Monthly view (per-service)​
searchDocs.content: A full-month grid view for a single service, showing all statuses side by side.

searchDocs.title: Daily tracking​
searchDocs.content: Tap any day to open the daily tracking panel. Each service shows its current status with one-tap buttons to change it.    After marking selections, the calendar grid updates immediately to reflect the logged statuses.

searchDocs.title: Date detail modal​
searchDocs.content: Click any date on the main calendar to see a combined summary of all service statuses for that day.

searchDocs.title: Service details panel​
searchDocs.content: The service details side panel appears when you tap a service name on the calendar. It shows the service's current settings, rates, and billing day.

searchDocs.title: Yearly view​
searchDocs.content: A 12-month summary grid showing payment activity across the full year. Useful for spotting gaps and verifying consistency.

searchDocs.title: Bills​
searchDocs.content: Bills are auto-generated every month based on your logged calendar activity and service settings. Every bill is detailed, trackable, and exportable.

searchDocs.title: Bill overview screen​
searchDocs.content: The Bills section lists all current-cycle bills with their amounts, services, and payment status.

searchDocs.title: Auto-generated bills​
searchDocs.content: Bills are generated automatically via a scheduled cron job at the start of each month. Each bill aggregates that month's tracked activity into a final amount.

searchDocs.title: Auto-bill notification email​
searchDocs.content: When a new monthly bill is generated, you receive an email notification summarising the charges.

searchDocs.title: AI-enhanced bill view​
searchDocs.content: When AI is configured, each bill displays an AI-generated summary card that explains the charges in plain English and highlights any notable changes.

searchDocs.title: Mark as paid​
searchDocs.content: Open the Mark as Paid modal on any bill to record the payment method (cash, UPI, bank transfer, etc.) and add optional notes.    After confirming, the bill status updates to Paid with a visible confirmation badge.

searchDocs.title: Bill history​
searchDocs.content: All past bills are archived and searchable. Filter by month, service, or payment status.

searchDocs.title: Export as PDF or CSV​
searchDocs.content: Download any bill as a formatted PDF for records, or export to CSV for spreadsheet analysis.

searchDocs.title: AI Features​
searchDocs.content: YesBill includes three distinct AI experiences: a persistent chat assistant, a floating action agent, and AI-generated content embedded throughout the app.

searchDocs.title: Ask AI — persistent chat​
searchDocs.content: Ask AI is a full chat interface with persistent conversation history. Ask it anything about your bills, services, spending habits, or household finances.

searchDocs.title: Conversation options & per-message features​
searchDocs.content: Each message has contextual actions — copy the response, provide thumbs-up/down feedback, and view per-message token usage and cost.

searchDocs.title: Model selection​
searchDocs.content: Switch between all available models from your configured AI provider. Each model is listed with its display name so you can choose the right tool for your query.

searchDocs.title: Reasoning model support​
searchDocs.content: For providers and models that support extended reasoning (e.g., OpenAI o-series, Anthropic Claude, Gemini with thinking), select the reasoning effort level — Low, Medium, or High — to control depth and cost.

searchDocs.title: Ask AI analytics​
searchDocs.content: The analytics panel shows cumulative token usage and cost across your Ask AI conversations, broken down per message.    When a reasoning model is in use, the analytics panel additionally shows thinking tokens and reasoning cost separately.

searchDocs.title: AI Agent — floating assistant​
searchDocs.content: The AI Agent is a floating, Intercom-style chatbot widget that lives on every screen. It can take actions in the app — creating services, logging calendar entries, looking up bills — with a confirmation step before committing any change.

searchDocs.title: Agent thinking mode​
searchDocs.content: When a reasoning-capable model is selected for the Agent, a Thinking indicator shows while the model reasons through multi-step tasks.

searchDocs.title: Agent analytics​
searchDocs.content: Token usage, reasoning tokens, cost, and latency for every Agent message are accessible from the analytics panel inside the Agent chat.

searchDocs.title: AI Configuration​
searchDocs.content: AI settings are available both during onboarding and at any time from Settings → AI Configuration.

searchDocs.title: 1 — Choose your provider​
searchDocs.content: Select from OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), or Google Gemini. Enter your API key for the selected provider.

searchDocs.title: 2 — Select a model​
searchDocs.content: Choose which model to use as the default for Ask AI and the Agent. All models available on your provider are listed.

searchDocs.title: 3 — Set default reasoning effort​
searchDocs.content: For reasoning-capable models, choose a default effort level — Low, Medium, or High. This becomes the default across all AI features and can be overridden per-session in Ask AI.

searchDocs.title: 4 — AI Insights toggle​
searchDocs.content: Enable or disable AI-generated bill summaries globally. When enabled, each new bill gets an automatically generated natural-language summary. When disabled, the AI Bill Summary card is hidden across all bills.    The same configuration is accessible from the Settings page.

searchDocs.title: Spend analytics​
searchDocs.content: The Analytics dashboard breaks down your total monthly spend by service type and individual service. See at a glance where your money goes each month.

searchDocs.title: AI usage analytics​
searchDocs.content: A dedicated AI usage panel tracks your total API consumption — input tokens, output tokens, reasoning tokens where applicable, total cost, and average latency per request.

searchDocs.title: Profile​
searchDocs.content: Update your display name, avatar photo, cover image, bio, and timezone from the Profile settings page.

searchDocs.title: Notifications​
searchDocs.content: Configure which events trigger in-app notifications. Preferences are per notification type — bill generated, bill due, payment confirmed, and more.    The notification panel shows recent activity and can be accessed from the header bell icon on any screen.

searchDocs.title: Security​
searchDocs.content: The Security settings page centralises all account security actions.    Change password​  Enter your current password and set a new one. A confirmation email is sent to your address after the change succeeds.      Change email address​  A three-email confirmation flow keeps your account secure:  Submit the new email address from Settings.A confirmation link is sent to your new email address — click it to approve the change.A notification email is sent to your old address so you are always informed if the change was not initiated by you.          Active sessions​  View all currently active login sessions for your account — browser, device type, and last active timestamp. Useful for spotting unexpected access.    Delete account​  Permanently delete your account and all associated data. A confirmation dialog requires you to acknowledge the action before it proceeds.      A final confirmation email is sent to confirm the account has been deleted.

searchDocs.title: Technical​
searchDocs.content: Backend: FastAPI + Python with Supabase (PostgreSQL + Row Level Security)Auth: Supabase Auth — email/password + Google OAuthFrontend: React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSSAI: Multi-provider — OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google GeminiRealtime: Supabase Realtime for live notification deliveryBill generation: Automated cron job via scheduled cloud taskDeployment: Netlify (frontend) + Render (backend)    Released March 2026 · YesBill v1.0.0

searchDocs.title: Common Examples​
searchDocs.content: 🥛 Morning milk (e.g. ₹28/litre × 2 litres = ₹56/day)📰 Daily newspaper🍱 Tiffin / lunch box service🍞 Bread or bakery delivery

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services → + Add ServiceSelect Home Delivery as the typeEnter: Service Name (e.g. "Morning Milk")Daily Rate in ₹ (e.g. 56)Start Date Save

searchDocs.title: Tracking Deliveries​
searchDocs.content: On the Calendar view, you'll see each day of the month. For each day:  Click Delivered (✅) if the service was deliveredClick Skipped (❌) if it was not delivered that day  Days default to Pending until you mark them.

searchDocs.title: Billing Calculation​
searchDocs.content: At month end:  Bill = Daily Rate × Number of Delivered Days   Example: Milk at ₹56/day, delivered 26 out of 30 days = ₹1,456  tip Mark skipped days promptly — once the month ends, past days may be locked from editing.

searchDocs.title: Manage Service View​
searchDocs.content: The Manage Service screen gives you a detailed overview of a single service, including:  Service name, type, and billing rateTotal amount billed this monthDays tracked and their statusesA quick link to the service's calendarOptions to Edit or Delete the service

searchDocs.title: How to Open​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services in the sidebarClick the menu icon (⋮) on any service cardSelect Manage — or simply click the service card itself

searchDocs.title: Editing a Service​
searchDocs.content: You can update any service's details at any time. Changes take effect from the next billing period unless otherwise noted.

searchDocs.title: How to Edit​
searchDocs.content: Open the Manage Service screen (see above)Click the Edit button (pencil icon or "Edit Service" button)The Edit Service form opens with current values pre-filledUpdate any of the following fields:  Field	NotesService Name	Rename the service as needed Service Type	Changing type may affect existing tracking data Daily Rate / Monthly Price	New rate applies to future bills Billing Day	Day of month when fixed charges are applied Start Date	When tracking began for this service Notes	Optional free-text notes about the service  Click Save Changes to apply  tip If you need to change just the price (e.g. your milk vendor raised rates), update the Daily Rate and click Save. Past bills will not be affected — only future months.  warning Changing the Service Type (e.g. from Home Delivery to Visit-based) will change how future days are tracked. Existing records for the current month will retain their original status labels.

searchDocs.title: Deleting a Service​
searchDocs.content: To permanently remove a service:  Open the Manage Service screenClick Delete ServiceConfirm in the dialog that appears  warning Deleting a service removes all associated tracking data and bills. This action cannot be undone. Consider marking the service as Inactive instead if you just want to pause it.

searchDocs.title: Pausing a Service​
searchDocs.content: For Utility type services, you can mark the service as Inactive for a month instead of deleting it. This means you won't be charged that month but the service remains configured.  For Home Delivery or Visit-based services, simply don't mark any days as Delivered/Visited — those days won't count toward your bill.

searchDocs.title: Services Page​
searchDocs.content: The Services page shows all your configured services at a glance. From here you can add new services, view any service's calendar, or manage and edit existing ones.

searchDocs.title: Service Cards​
searchDocs.content: Each service is displayed as a card showing its name, type, rate, and current month's status. Click a card to open that service's calendar view, or use the menu (⋮) to edit or delete it.

searchDocs.title: Home Delivery​
searchDocs.content: Daily services delivered to your door. Tracked day-by-day as Delivered or Skipped.  Examples: Milk, newspaper, tiffin, bread, vegetables  Billing: Daily rate × number of days delivered

searchDocs.title: Visit-based​
searchDocs.content: Services where someone visits your home. Tracked day-by-day as Visited or Missed.  Examples: Maid, cook, driver, gardener, watchman  Billing: Daily rate × number of days visited

searchDocs.title: Utility​
searchDocs.content: Monthly services that are either active or inactive for the whole month.  Examples: Internet, DTH/cable, electricity (flat rate), piped gas  Billing: Full monthly price if marked Active, ₹0 if marked Inactive

searchDocs.title: Subscription​
searchDocs.content: Fixed-charge services billed on a specific day each month.  Examples: Netflix, Spotify, Gym membership, OTT platforms  Billing: Fixed price on the billing day, regardless of usage

searchDocs.title: Payment​
searchDocs.content: Loan repayments or credit obligations tracked monthly.  Examples: EMI, credit card minimum due, personal loan  Billing: Fixed amount due on the billing day

searchDocs.title: Comparison Table​
searchDocs.content: Feature	Home Delivery	Visit-based	Utility	Subscription	PaymentDay-by-day tracking	Yes	Yes	—	—	— Monthly toggle	—	—	Yes	—	— Fixed monthly charge	—	—	—	Yes	Yes Language	Delivered/Skipped	Visited/Missed	Active/Inactive	Paid/Unpaid	Paid/Unpaid

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: For a service that comes some days but not every day (e.g. a part-time maid), use Visit-basedFor a flat ₹500/month internet connection, use Utility — toggle it Active when the connection is activeFor an EMI that is ₹7,500/month due on the 5th, use Payment with billing day = 5

searchDocs.title: Common Examples​
searchDocs.content: 🧹 Maid / domestic helper👨‍🍳 Cook🚗 Driver🌱 Gardener / mali👮 Watchman (part-time)

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services → + Add ServiceSelect Visit-based as the typeEnter: Service Name (e.g. "Maid")Daily Rate in ₹Start Date Save

searchDocs.title: Tracking Visits​
searchDocs.content: On the Calendar view, for each day:  Click Visited (✅) if the person came that dayClick Missed (❌) if they did not come  note Visit-based uses "Visited/Missed" language — not "Delivered/Skipped" — because a person visits rather than a product being delivered.

searchDocs.title: Billing Calculation​
searchDocs.content: Bill = Daily Rate × Number of Visited Days   Example: Maid at ₹200/day, came 22 out of 26 working days = ₹4,400

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: If the maid has fixed off days (Sunday), simply don't mark those days — they'll remain Pending and won't be countedAt month end, you can compare "visited" vs "missed" to track attendance easily

searchDocs.title: Common Examples​
searchDocs.content: Netflix, Prime Video, HotstarSpotify, Apple Music, YouTube MusicGym / fitness centre membershipMagazine or news subscriptionCloud storage (Google One, iCloud)

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services → + Add ServiceSelect Subscription as the typeEnter: Service Name (e.g. "Netflix")Monthly Price in ₹Billing Day — the day of the month this is due (e.g. 15) Save

searchDocs.title: Tracking​
searchDocs.content: On the Calendar, the subscription appears only on its billing day as a Paid or Unpaid status badge.  Click Mark Paid when you've paid for the monthThe payment is recorded with your chosen payment method

searchDocs.title: Billing​
searchDocs.content: Subscriptions contribute their fixed monthly amount to your bill each month, regardless of usage.  Bill = Fixed Monthly Price   note Subscription and Payment service types both work the same way — fixed charge on a specific billing day.

searchDocs.title: Common Examples​
searchDocs.content: Internet / broadbandDTH / cable TVPiped gas (flat-rate plan)Electricity (flat monthly plan)

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services → + Add ServiceSelect Utility as the typeEnter: Service Name (e.g. "Internet")Monthly Rate in ₹Billing Day (e.g. 1 for 1st of each month) Save

searchDocs.title: Tracking​
searchDocs.content: Unlike Home Delivery or Visit-based, Utility services don't have a day-by-day grid. Instead, on the Service Calendar page:  Mark Active — Service is running this month, full charge appliesMark Inactive — Service is paused/cancelled, ₹0 charge this month

searchDocs.title: Billing Calculation​
searchDocs.content: Bill = Monthly Rate (if Active) Bill = ₹0 (if Inactive)   Example: Internet plan at ₹799/month, marked Active = ₹799 in the bill  tip If your internet was down for a week and you got a credit, you can still mark it Active and add a note to the bill manually.

searchDocs.title: Common Examples​
searchDocs.content: Home loan / car loan EMICredit card minimum payment duePersonal loan repaymentSchool/college fee (monthly instalment)

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Go to Services → + Add ServiceSelect Payment as the typeEnter: Service Name (e.g. "Car Loan EMI")Monthly Amount in ₹ (e.g. 12,500)Billing Day (e.g. 7 for 7th of each month) Save

searchDocs.title: Tracking​
searchDocs.content: On the Calendar, the payment appears only on its billing day showing a Paid or Unpaid status.  When you pay:  Click Mark Paid on the calendar or in the Bills sectionSelect your payment method (Bank Transfer, UPI, Cash, etc.)Optionally add a payment note (e.g. transaction reference)

searchDocs.title: Notes​
searchDocs.content: Payment type is identical to Subscription in how it's tracked — both are fixed charges on a billing dayThe distinction is semantic — use Payment for financial obligations (loans, dues) and Subscription for services (streaming, memberships)Past payment history is stored so you can verify you've never missed an EMI

searchDocs.title: Accessing AI Settings​
searchDocs.content: Go to Settings → AI Configuration tab.

searchDocs.title: Provider Tabs​
searchDocs.content: Click the provider name (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) to switch between them.

searchDocs.title: Adding a Key​
searchDocs.content: Select the provider tabPaste your API key in the input fieldChoose your preferred model from the dropdownClick Validate — YesBill sends a test request to verify the key worksClick Save  A green checkmark (✅) appears next to a validated provider.

searchDocs.title: Removing a Key​
searchDocs.content: Open the provider tabClick Remove Key or clear the fieldSave

searchDocs.title: Default Model​
searchDocs.content: The model you select here becomes the default in Ask AI and the Agent. You can always change it per-conversation using the model selector in the chat interface.

searchDocs.title: Model Capabilities​
searchDocs.content: Some models support Extended Thinking (reasoning) — these show a 🧠 icon in the model selector. Reasoning models think through problems step by step before responding, which can improve accuracy for complex queries.

searchDocs.title: Usage Monitoring​
searchDocs.content: Go to Analytics → AI Usage to see:  Tokens used per conversationCost per messageTotal monthly AI spend  This helps you estimate your AI provider bill.  tip For everyday use, GPT-4o Mini (OpenAI) or Gemini 2.0 Flash (Google) are excellent budget-friendly choices. Switch to a more powerful model for complex analysis tasks.

searchDocs.title: Accessing Notification Settings​
searchDocs.content: Go to Settings → Notifications tabToggle each notification type on or off

searchDocs.title: Notification Types​
searchDocs.content: Type	What Triggers ItGeneral	Welcome messages and announcements Bill Generated	When a new bill is created for any service Bill Warning	When bills are nearing due date Service Expiry	When a service is about to end AI Config	Reminder when no AI provider is configured Email Verification	Security emails (email change, password reset)

searchDocs.title: Reading Notifications​
searchDocs.content: Click the bell icon (🔔) in the top navbar to open the notification panel:  Unread notifications have a blue dot (●)Click Mark all as read to clear unread indicatorsClick Manage notification preferences to jump to Settings

searchDocs.title: Tips​
searchDocs.content: Disable notification types you don't find useful — only keep the ones that matter to youBill Generated notifications are the most useful — they remind you to review and payThe notification panel shows the last 50 notifications. Older ones are not shown but are still in the database.

searchDocs.title: Accessing Profile Settings​
searchDocs.content: Click your name/avatar in the top rightSelect Settings from the dropdownYou're on the Profile tab by default

searchDocs.title: Editing Your Profile​
searchDocs.content: Click Edit Profile to enter edit mode where you can update all fields. Changes are saved when you click Save Changes.

searchDocs.title: Basic Information​
searchDocs.content: Full Name — Your legal or preferred full nameDisplay Name — Short name used in greetings and the app headerPhone Number — Optional, used for WhatsApp notificationsBio — A short description (up to 500 characters)Country & Timezone — Used for correct date display

searchDocs.title: Profile Photo (Avatar)​
searchDocs.content: Click on your avatar circleSelect Upload PhotoChoose an image from your deviceThe photo is uploaded and saved automatically

searchDocs.title: Cover Image​
searchDocs.content: A banner image that appears at the top of your profile card.  Click Upload Cover Image (or the camera icon on the cover area)Choose a wide image (recommended: 1200×300 px or wider)Save

searchDocs.title: Saving Changes​
searchDocs.content: Click Save Changes after editing. A success toast appears confirming the save.  tip Add a recognisable profile photo and display name — it makes the app feel more personal.

searchDocs.title: Opening Support​
searchDocs.content: Click Support in the sidebar to open the support centre. From here you can choose the type of request you want to submit.

searchDocs.title: Raise a Bug​
searchDocs.content: Use this form to report something that isn't working correctly:  Describe the bug — What happened? What did you expect to happen?Steps to reproduce — How can we replicate it?Severity — How badly does this affect your usage?Click Submit — the report is sent to the YesBill team  tip The more detail you provide, the faster we can fix it. Include the page you were on and any error messages you saw.

searchDocs.title: Request a Feature​
searchDocs.content: Have an idea that would make YesBill better?  Feature title — Short description of what you wantDescribe the feature — How should it work? What problem does it solve?Priority — How important is this to you?Click Submit

searchDocs.title: AI Feature Issue​
searchDocs.content: Use this form specifically for issues related to the AI features (Ask AI, AI bill summaries, AI agent):  Which AI feature? — Select the feature that had the issueDescribe the issue — What response was wrong or unhelpful?Click Submit  This helps us improve the AI prompts and context accuracy.

searchDocs.title: Response Times​
searchDocs.content: The YesBill team reviews support tickets regularly. You'll receive a response via the email address on your account.

searchDocs.title: Accessing Security Settings​
searchDocs.content: Go to Settings → Security tab.

searchDocs.title: Changing Your Password​
searchDocs.content: Enter your Current PasswordEnter a New Password (must meet strength requirements)Confirm the new passwordClick Update Password  Password requirements:  At least 8 charactersAt least one uppercase letterAt least one lowercase letterAt least one numberAt least one symbol

searchDocs.title: Password Changed Email​
searchDocs.content: After a successful password change, YesBill sends a confirmation email to your registered address notifying you that the password was updated. If you didn't make this change, contact support immediately.  warning After changing your password, all other active sessions will be signed out.

searchDocs.title: Changing Your Email Address​
searchDocs.content: Enter your New Email Address in the email fieldClick Send ConfirmationCheck your new email inbox for a confirmation linkClick the link — you'll be taken to a confirmation pageYour email is updated and you're redirected to login

searchDocs.title: Email Change — Step by Step Emails​
searchDocs.content: YesBill sends three emails during an email change:  1. Confirmation email to your new address​    Click the Confirm Email Change button in this email to verify you own the new address.  2. In-app notification after change​    Once the change completes, you'll see an in-app notification confirming your email address has been updated.  3. Confirmation email to your old address​    Your old email address also receives a notification confirming the change. If you didn't request this, contact support immediately.  note A security notification email is also sent to your old email address when the change is complete.

searchDocs.title: If the Link Expires​
searchDocs.content: Email change links expire after 24 hours. If yours expired:  Go back to Settings → SecurityRe-enter your new email and click Send Confirmation againA fresh confirmation link is emailed to you

searchDocs.title: Active Sessions​
searchDocs.content: The Active Sessions panel shows your currently logged-in devices and browsers. You can see:  Device type and browserLast active time and locationCurrent session badge  To sign out of all other sessions, click Sign Out All Other Sessions. This is useful if you think someone else might have access to your account.

searchDocs.title: Deleting Your Account​
searchDocs.content: danger Account deletion is permanent and irreversible. All your services, tracking data, and bills will be deleted immediately and cannot be recovered.

searchDocs.title: Step 1 — Initiate Deletion​
searchDocs.content: Scroll to the bottom of the Security settings page and click Delete Account.

searchDocs.title: Step 2 — Confirm Deletion​
searchDocs.content: A confirmation dialog appears. You must type your account email address to confirm before the deletion proceeds.

searchDocs.title: Account Deletion Email​
searchDocs.content: After your account is deleted, you'll receive a final confirmation email notifying you that the deletion is complete and all data has been removed.

searchDocs.title: Linked Accounts​
searchDocs.content: If you signed up with Google, your email is managed by Google. You cannot change it directly in YesBill — change it in your Google Account settings instead.
